descriptions: Bahala Na Gang was formed to counter the Sige-Sige Gang, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Another gang tried to take control: the Sige-Sige Gang. ... To counter the existing Sige-Sige Gang, Divino formed the Bahala Na Gang

Mickey Cobras.description: The Mickey Cobras are an African American street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, founded in 1954 as the Egyptian Cobras by James Cogwell. The gang is affiliated with the People Nation alliance and is considered stable, with colors green, black, and red. It was renamed in honor of Henry 'Mickey' Cogwell after his assassination in 1977. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder, with territory in Chicago and the Midwest.

descriptions: Mickey Cobras are a splinter group that split from BPSN.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mickey Cobras were supporters of Mickey Cogwell, a co-founder of BPSN killed by Jeff Fort.

descriptions: Mickey Cobras is listed as an ally of Almighty Vice Lord Nation.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black P. Stones , Bloods , Latin Kings , People Nation , Mickey Cobras

descriptions: Mickey Cobras are listed as rivals of Black Disciples.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples ( conflict ) , Vice Lords , Black P. Stones , Mickey Cobras

descriptions: Mickey Cobras is listed as a rival of Gangster Disciples in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black P. Stones , Black Disciples ( conflict ) , Bloods , Latin Kings , People Nation , Vice Lords , Mickey Cobras

descriptions: Infobox lists Mickey Cobras as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Latin Kings, Spanish Cobras, Latin Eagles, Four Corner Hustlers, Black P. Stones, Simon City Royals, Ambrose, Vice Lords, Gaylords, Almighty Saints, La Raza, Mickey Cobras

descriptions: The Egyptian Cobras fought with the 14th Street Clovers in the mid-1950s.
edges.evidence_quote: During the mid-1950s the Egyptian Cobras fought heavily with a gang called the 14th Street Clovers.

descriptions: The Egyptian Cobras (later Cobras Stones) allied with Black P. Stone Nation against common enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1966 the Cobras new leader Henry (Mickey) Cogwell held second highest position in the Main 21 alliance, comprising the leaders of different gangs within the Black P. Stone Nation (BPSN). The Cobras and the Black P. Stone Nation allied against the Disciple Alliance and later the Gangster Nation.

descriptions: The Vice Lords became dominant, causing the Egyptian Cobras to relocate.
edges.evidence_quote: When the Vice Lords returned to Lawndale in the year 1958, they soon swelled into a force to be reckoned with. The Vice Lords were very aggressive and the dominant gang in Lawndale. By 1960 the Egyptian Cobras sought better opportunities on the South Side of Chicago.

descriptions: The Black Disciples are rivals of the Mickey Cobras.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of their rivals would be Gangster Disciples , Black Disciples

descriptions: Mickey Cogwell was portrayed as a link between the Mickey Cobras and the Chicago Outfit.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1970, the commander of the Gang Intelligence Unit for the Chicago Police Department portrayed Cogwell as the link between gangs and organized crime , particularly the Chicago Outfit .

descriptions: The Mickey Cobras are rivals of the Folk Nation alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: People Nation , Chicago Outfit; Rivals: Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , Black Disciples

descriptions: The Gangster Disciples are rivals of the Mickey Cobras.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of their rivals would be Gangster Disciples

descriptions: The Mickey Cobras are a member of the People Nation alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mickey Cobras are a gang based in Chicago and affiliated with People Nation.

descriptions: Mickey Cobras are an initial member of the People Nation.
edges.evidence_quote: Among initial members to the People were the Mickey Cobras (then named the Cobra Stones), Bishops, Spanish Lords, soon after the Gaylords and the Insane South Side Popes.

descriptions: Jackson-Side Wah Ching is an ally of Tokyo Boyz.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Jackson-Side Wah Ching

descriptions: Omi-ikka is listed as an ally of Kodo-kai in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Seibu Rengo, Komatsu-gumi, Koike-gumi, Fukushima Rengo, Jido-kai, Omi-ikka

descriptions: The Beehive Boys and South Side Stickers cooperated to confront police.
edges.evidence_quote: the Beehive Boys and the South Side Stickers reportedly joined forces to confront police officers who were taking two prisoners to the police station.

descriptions: The Black Flag Army was formed from former Taiping soldiers.
edges.evidence_quote: was a splinter remnant of a bandit and mercenary group recruited largely from soldiers of ethnic Zhuang background and former Taiping soldiers

descriptions: The Charles Birger gang and Shelton Brothers Gang together fought the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang and rival Charles Birger gang engaged in a war with the Ku Klux Klan that concluded with a shoot out at Herrin.

descriptions: The Shelton Brothers Gang and Charles Birger gang engaged in a war.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang engaged in a war with Charles Birger 's gang that concluded, in 1925, when the Shelton Brothers were convicted of the murder of a mail carrier.

10th & Oregon Crew.description: Predominantly Italian American organized crime gang based in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, founded in the 1960s by Rocco Turra. Active in South Philadelphia and South Jersey, the gang engages in drug trafficking, gambling, extortion, and loan sharking. It is associated with but independent from the Philadelphia crime family, with periods of both alliance and violent feuding. The gang remains active as of the present.

descriptions: The 10th & Oregon Crew had multiple violent conflicts with the rival 12th & Wharton gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the 10th & Oregon Crew had multiple run-ins with a rival street gang, 12th & Wharton.

descriptions: The 10th & Oregon Crew has established relations with the Narducci Crew, a part of the Philadelphia Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang has established relations with the Philadelphia Mafia's Narducci Crew.

descriptions: The 10th & Oregon Crew rivaled the Pagans Motorcycle Club over drug and loansharking territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The 10th & Oregon Crew rivaled the Pagans for drug dealing and loansharking territory.

descriptions: Pagans attacked 10th & Oregon Crew members in a bar, escalating the conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On February 28, 1999, five members of the 10th & Oregon Crew were beaten when a group of Pagan bikers stormed a South Philadelphia bar owned by Miller

descriptions: A peace meeting between the 10th & Oregon Crew and Pagans failed to resolve the dispute.
edges.evidence_quote: No agreement was reached, however, and the dispute between the gangs persisted.

descriptions: Pagans president Mondevergine was ambushed and shot, likely by 10th & Oregon Crew members.
edges.evidence_quote: On August 29, 1999, Mondevergine survived being shot eight times at close range when he was ambushed as he walked to his mother's house after leaving a bar.

descriptions: Pagans president Mondevergine shot at 10th & Oregon Crew member Hendri in retaliation.
edges.evidence_quote: On November 3, 2000, Mondevergine retaliated by opening fire on Hendri as he left the Oregon Diner, firing twice and missing.

descriptions: The infobox lists Pagan's MC as a rival of the 10th & Oregon Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Pagan's MC

descriptions: The 10th & Oregon Crew has both allied and feuded with the Philadelphia crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Under different leadership, the group has both been allied closely with and violently feuded against the Philadelphia crime family at various times.

descriptions: 10th & Oregon Crew members assaulted Philadelphia crime family associates in 2002.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 2002, three Philadelphia crime family associates were reportedly beaten and knocked unconscious by 10th & Oregon Crew members after a verbal disagreement at a club on Delaware Avenue in Penn's Landing.

descriptions: The 10th & Oregon Crew has been both allied and feuded with the Philadelphia crime family at different times.
edges.evidence_quote: Under different leadership, the group has both been allied closely with and violently feuded against the Philadelphia crime family at various times.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Philadelphia crime family as an ally of the 10th & Oregon Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: The 10th & Oregon Crew is associated with but independent from the Philadelphia crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang is associated with but independent from the Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: The American Mafia is allied with the 10th & Oregon Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sicilian Mafia , Camorra , 'Ndrangheta , various independent Italian-American gangs (such as the 10th & Oregon Crew , Purple Gang , and Tanglewood Boys )

descriptions: The American Mafia lists the 10th & Oregon Crew as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Sicilian Mafia , Camorra , 'Ndrangheta , Sacra corona unita , varias pandillas independientes ítaloestadounidenses (como la 10th & Oregon Crew , Purple Gang , South Brooklyn Boys , y Tanglewood Boys ) , mafia judía , mafia griega , mafia corsa , varias pandillas de motociclistas (como los Hells Angels , Outlaws y Pagans ) , varias pandillas callejeras (tales como los Latin Kings ) , ocasionalmente la mafia albanesa y la mafia rusa

descriptions: American Mafia lists 10th & Oregon Crew as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... varias pandillas independientes ítaloestadounidenses (como la 10th & Oregon Crew , Purple Gang , South Brooklyn Boys , y Tanglewood Boys)

descriptions: The American Mafia is allied with the 10th & Oregon Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... varias pandillas independientes ítaloestadounidenses (como la 10th & Oregon Crew, Purple Gang, South Brooklyn Boys, y Tanglewood Boys)

descriptions: Merlino's men beat the leader of the 10th & Oregon Crew for failing to pay a street tax.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Louis Turra, leader of a Philadelphia drug gang, the 10th & Oregon Crew, was severely beaten by Merlino's men, allegedly for failing to pay a Mafia street tax on the gang's illegal earnings.

descriptions: Infobox lists 10th & Oregon Crew as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... 10th & Oregon Crew ...

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 10th & Oregon Crew

descriptions: The infobox lists the 10th & Oregon Crew as an ally of the Philadelphia crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bufalino crime family, DeCavalcante crime family, Detroit Partnership, Gambino crime family, Genovese crime family, Lucchese crime family, Patriarca crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, 10th & Oregon Crew, Junior Black Mafia, K&A Gang, Pagan's MC, Philadelphia Greek Mob, Warlocks MC

descriptions: The infobox lists the 10th & Oregon Crew as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 10th & Oregon Crew

descriptions: The San Giuseppe Jato Mafia family was part of the Corleonesi coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: Giovanni Brusca from San Giuseppe Jato

Nacionalni stroj.description: Neo-Nazi organization in Serbia, based in the Vojvodina region. Orchestrated several incidents since 2005. Banned in Serbia in 2011 after charges were pressed against leading members.

descriptions: Infobox lists BDM as an ally of TCP.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: BDM

descriptions: Justin Street Locos was a historical street clique of Westside Locos.
edges.evidence_quote: It historically had other street clicks in Glendale such as justin street locos

descriptions: Infobox lists Gente Nueva de Agua Prieta as an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Gente Nueva de Agua Prieta

Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington.description: An outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1945 in Bloomington, California by Norman Briggs. It participated in the 1947 Hollister riot that inspired the film The Wild One. The club is still active, with chapters in California, Nevada, and Utah. Its colors are red and blue, and its motto is PFFP.

descriptions: Both clubs participated together in the 1947 Hollister incident.
edges.evidence_quote: The Market Street Commandos was an outlaw motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington , participated in the highly publicized Hollister incident

descriptions: POBOB and Boozefighters jointly participated in the Hollister riot in 1947.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington (POBOB) is a motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Market Street Commandos , participated in the highly publicized Hollister riot

descriptions: Hells Angels was founded by a former POBOB member who broke away from POBOB over a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: One former Pissed Off Bastards member, Otto Friedli, is credited with founding the Hells Angels in Fontana after breaking from the POBOB over a feud with a rival gang.

descriptions: POBOB and Market Street Commandos jointly participated in the Hollister riot in 1947.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington (POBOB) is a motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Market Street Commandos , participated in the highly publicized Hollister riot

'Ndrina Crea.description: The Crea are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Rizziconi, Calabria, Italy. They have been involved in organized crime activities including drug trafficking and extortion. Key members include Teodoro Crea, arrested in 2006, and Giuseppe Crea, arrested in 2016. The clan has been targeted in multiple police operations and remains active.

descriptions: Alvaro and Crea were both implicated in arrests in Rizziconi.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 4 giugno 2014 a Rizziconi, su denuncia del sindaco e dopo due anni di indagini vengono arrestate 16 persone riconducibile alle 'ndrine Crea ed Alvaro.

descriptions: Crea helped Bellocco's boss reassert leadership.
edges.evidence_quote: Umberto Bellocco viene scarcerato il 14 aprile 2014 ... avrebbe tentato di riaffermare la propria leadership, con l'aiuto dei Crea , e dei suoi familiari.

descriptions: Crea and Alvaro are both implicated in the same 2014 arrests, suggesting cooperation or shared criminal activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 4 giugno 2014 a Rizziconi, su denuncia del sindaco e dopo due anni di indagini vengono arrestate 16 persone riconducibile alle 'ndrine Crea ed Alvaro.

descriptions: Crea, Molè, and Pesce are jointly targeted in the same 2021 police operation, indicating they are part of a common criminal network.
edges.evidence_quote: 16 novembre 2021: operazione "Nuova Narcos Europea" contro i Molè, i Pesce e i Crea.

descriptions: Crea, Pesce, and Molè are jointly targeted in the same 2021 police operation, indicating they are part of a common criminal network.
edges.evidence_quote: 16 novembre 2021: operazione "Nuova Narcos Europea" contro i Molè, i Pesce e i Crea.

descriptions: Novella mediated peace between Gallace and Crea di Siderno.
edges.evidence_quote: Carmelo Novella fece da paciere tra i Gallace e i Crea di Siderno invocando l'aiuto di Antonio Pelle.

descriptions: Luciano Ursino of Lo Presti entered business relations with the Crea brothers.
edges.evidence_quote: Luciano Ursino, entra in rapporti di affari con i fratelli Adolfo e Aldo Cosimo Crea, originari di Stilo , in Calabria , boss emergenti della 'Ndrangheta torinese.

descriptions: The Molè, Pesce, and Crea were targeted together in an operation, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: 16 novembre 2021: operazione Nuova Narcos Europea contro i Molè, i Pesce e i Crea .

descriptions: Pesce and Crea were targeted together in operation Nuova Narcos Europea.
edges.evidence_quote: 16 novembre 2021: operazione "Nuova Narcos Europea" contro i Molè , i Pesce e i Crea .

descriptions: Ascone and Crea were opposing sides in the Faida di Rizziconi.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Rizziconi , esplosa tra gli Ascone e i Crea .

descriptions: The Taliban fought against the Islamic State of Afghanistan.
edges.evidence_quote: shifting power away from the Islamic State of Afghanistan

descriptions: Taliban opposed and displaced the Islamic State of Afghanistan.
edges.evidence_quote: shifting power away from the Islamic State of Afghanistan

descriptions: The Taliban fought against and displaced the Islamic State of Afghanistan.
edges.evidence_quote: the movement spread through most of Afghanistan, shifting power away from the Islamic State of Afghanistan

edges.evidence_quote: In 2020, Kata'ib Hezbollah, Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba, Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq and Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada formed the Islamic Resistance in Iraq.

descriptions: Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada is a member group of PMF.
edges.evidence_quote: Kata’ib Sayyid al-Shuhada, a member of the PMF, stated that their forces were bombed by US planes on 7 August 2017.

descriptions: El Forastero was formed after being rejected for a chapter by Satan Slaves MC.
edges.evidence_quote: El Forastero Motorcycle Club ( EFMC ) is a one-percenter motorcycle club which was established after being turned down for a chapter by the Satan Slaves MC.

Bugs and Meyer Mob.description: Jewish-American street gang based in Manhattan's Lower East Side, formed in the 1920s by Bugsy Siegel and Meyer Lansky. Active during Prohibition, the gang engaged in bootlegging, truck hijacking, murder, and illegal gambling. It served as a predecessor to Murder, Inc. and worked closely with Charles Luciano and Frank Costello.

descriptions: Bugs and Meyer Mob worked with Broadway Mob in the 1920s.
edges.evidence_quote: Lansky and Siegel, being longtime associates of Luciano, would frequently employ the gang to work with Joe Adonis's Broadway Mob throughout the 1920s.

descriptions: Bugs and Meyer Mob was a predecessor to Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bugs and Meyer mob acted as a predecessor to Murder, Inc.

descriptions: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was absorbed into Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: the Jewish Bugs and Meyer Mob operated on the Lower East Side of Manhattan before being absorbed into Murder, Inc.

descriptions: Bugs and Meyer Mob was the predecessor to Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was the predecessor to Murder, Incorporated.

descriptions: Bugs and Meyer Mob founders helped form the National Crime Syndicate and contributed to its enforcement arm.
edges.evidence_quote: When Lansky and Luciano formed the National Crime Syndicate in the early 1930s, Lansky, along with Siegel, pushed for a special outfit to handle 'enforcement,' or murders for the entire syndicate.

Rocks Push.description: A larrikin gang active in The Rocks area of Sydney from the 1870s to the 1890s. Engaged in theft, murder, assault, and robbery, often targeting drunks and seamen. Known for a distinctive dress style and bare-knuckle boxing leadership contests.

descriptions: Rocks Push was in running warfare with Argyle Cut Push.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: Rocks Push was in running warfare with Forty Thieves.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: Rocks Push was in running warfare with Gibb Street Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: Rocks Push was in running warfare with Glebe Push.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: Rocks Push was in running warfare with Miller's Point Push.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: Rocks Push was in running warfare with Straw Hat Push.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: The Abdel-Kader family cooperates with Jewish crime families, including the Amir Molnar syndicate.
edges.evidence_quote: Clans like Tayibe based Abdel-Kader family are involved in extortion, drug and weapon trafficking, fraud as well as money laundering, often in cooperation with their Jewish counterparts.

Tongan Crip Gang.description: The Tongan Crip Gang (TCG) is a Tongan American street gang and a subset of the Crips alliance, founded in Inglewood, California. It is active in California, Utah, Alaska, Washington, and parts of Auckland, New Zealand. The gang engages in a wide range of criminal activities including drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, murder, and fraud. It was involved in a 2007 conspiracy to murder members of MS-13.

descriptions: Tongan Crip Gang and 18th Street Gang conspired together to murder MS-13 members.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2007, members of the Tongan Crip Gang and the 18 Street Gang were indicted by a federal grand jury for criminal conspiracy in a plot to murder 33 members of a Los Angeles subset of MS-13.

descriptions: Baby Regulators is listed as a rival of Tongan Crip Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kearns Town Bloods, Baby Regulators, Lennox 13 Park Village Compton Crips, Carver Park Crips

descriptions: Carver Park Crips is listed as a rival of Tongan Crip Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kearns Town Bloods, Baby Regulators, Lennox 13 Park Village Compton Crips, Carver Park Crips

descriptions: Tongan Crip Gang is a subgroup of the Crips alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tongan Crip Gang ( TCG ) is a Tongan American street gang that is a subgroup, or " set ", of the Crips gang alliance.

descriptions: Kearns Town Bloods is listed as a rival of Tongan Crip Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kearns Town Bloods, Baby Regulators, Lennox 13 Park Village Compton Crips, Carver Park Crips

descriptions: Lennox 13 Park Village Compton Crips is listed as a rival of Tongan Crip Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kearns Town Bloods, Baby Regulators, Lennox 13 Park Village Compton Crips, Carver Park Crips

descriptions: Maggapa was a copycat gang inspired by the Chijon Family.
edges.evidence_quote: At the same time as the 1996 North Korean commandos infiltration incident , there was a South Korean nine-member copycat gang inspired by the Chijon Family. The copycat gang called themselves the Maggapa (막가파).

descriptions: La Familia Michoacana acted against the Valencia Family, expelling them.

descriptions: Hamas clashed with Jund Ansar Allah, resulting in deaths on both sides.
edges.evidence_quote: On 14 August 2009, Hamas fighters stormed the Mosque of extremist cleric Abdel-Latif Moussa ... The resulting battle left at least 13 people dead, including Moussa and six Hamas fighters, and 120 people injured.

descriptions: Jund Ansar Allah has links to Al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Jund Ansar Allah ("Army of the Helpers of God"), an Islamist group with links to Al-Qaeda.

descriptions: The Mokotów group splintered off from the Pruszków mafia and acted independently.
edges.evidence_quote: a group originating from the Pruszków mafia, known as the Mokotów group, splintered off and continued to act independently until 2014.

descriptions: White Hand Gang was rival to Italian gangsters.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Hand gangs , Italian gangsters

Rockers Motor Club.description: Canadian outlaw biker gang and support club for the Hells Angels, founded in 1992 by Maurice 'Mom' Boucher in Montreal. It served as an enforcement arm and recruitment tool for the Hells Angels during the Quebec Biker War, carrying out drug trafficking, assaults, and murders. The group was active until 2001.

descriptions: Rockers Motor Club is a puppet club of Hells Angels in Montreal.
edges.evidence_quote: Every Quebec region had its own puppet club: the Rockers Motor Club in Montreal, the Rowdy Ones in Sorel, the Evil Ones in Drummondville, the Satan's Guard in the Saguenay region, and the Jokers in St-Jean

descriptions: Rockers were a puppet club of the Hells Angels in Montreal.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Stadnick had the Angels' puppet club in Montreal, the Rockers, set up a puppet club in Winnipeg called the Redliners.

descriptions: Rockers are a puppet gang for Hells Angels Montreal.
edges.evidence_quote: two members of the Hells Angels Montreal puppet gang, the Rockers

descriptions: Rockers is a puppet club of Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: the Hells Angels created a puppet club, the Rockers Motor Club

descriptions: Rockers are a puppet club of Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Wooley was president of the Rockers, the Angels' puppet club in Montreal

descriptions: The Rockers were a puppet gang of the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: all of the Rockers and all of the Evil Ones puppet gangs

descriptions: Rockers MC was a support club for Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: It was created to counter Hell's Angels allies, the Rockers MC, Evil ones and Death Riders MC support clubs.

descriptions: Bandidos MC listed as a rival of Rockers MC in infobox (2001).
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Rock Machine MC Palmers MC , SS Elite MC , Dark Circle , Bandidos MC (2001)

descriptions: Dark Circle listed as a rival of Rockers MC in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Rock Machine MC Palmers MC , SS Elite MC , Dark Circle , Bandidos MC (2001)

descriptions: Death Riders MC assimilated into Rockers MC to form a new chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: a longstanding HAMC-affiliated outlaw motorcycle club based in Laval, Quebec known as the Death Riders MC assimilated into a new chapter of the Rockers Motor Club which became known as Rockers MC North.

descriptions: Evil Ones MC listed as an ally of Rockers MC in infobox.

descriptions: Rockers MC was a support club for Hells Angels, carrying out their unlawful objectives.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rockers Motor Club , often abbreviated as the Rockers MC , was a Canadian outlaw biker gang and support club for the larger Hells Angels Motorcycle Club .

descriptions: Rockers MC founded Redliners to oppose Los Brovos.
edges.evidence_quote: The resulting one-percenter supporter club that came about was the Redliners, which Stadnick plan ned to oppose two of Western Canada's dominant biker gangs: the Los Brovos and the Spartans MC.

descriptions: Rockers established the Redliners as a puppet club in Winnipeg.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Stadnick had the Angels' puppet club in Montreal, the Rockers, set up a puppet club in Winnipeg called the Redliners.

descriptions: Rockers MC founded the Redliners as an auxiliary club.
edges.evidence_quote: Walter "Nurget" Stadnick instructed the Rockers Motor Club to found an auxiliary outlaw motorcycle club of their own which would be based out of out west in Winnipeg, Manitoba . The resulting one-percenter supporter club that came about was the Redliners

descriptions: Rockers MC was involved in the war against Rock Machine on behalf of Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: In amidst of the war with Rock Machine, Rockers MC biker Paul "Fon Fon" Fontaine, along with an accomplice, murdered provincial prison guard Pierre Rondeau ...

descriptions: Rockers MC founded Redliners to oppose Spartans MC.
edges.evidence_quote: The resulting one-percenter supporter club that came about was the Redliners, which Stadnick plan ned to oppose two of Western Canada's dominant biker gangs: the Los Brovos and the Spartans MC.

descriptions: Both are members of the Northern Alliance, a coalition of insurgent groups.
edges.evidence_quote: the Northern Alliance —which consists of four insurgent groups , the Arakan Army (AA), the Kachin Independence Army (KIA), the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA) and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA)

descriptions: MNDAA succeeded CPB control in Kokang area after CPB disbanded.
edges.evidence_quote: From the 1960s to 1989, the Kokang area ... was controlled by the Communist Party of Burma, and after the party's armed wing disbanded in 1989 it became a special region ... under the control of the Myanmar Nationalities Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA)

descriptions: Both are members of the Northern Alliance, a coalition of insurgent groups.
edges.evidence_quote: the Northern Alliance —which consists of four insurgent groups , the Arakan Army (AA), the Kachin Independence Army (KIA), the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA) and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA)

descriptions: MNDAA attacked TNLA, breaking their alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In mid-March 2026, the alliance between MNDAA and TNLA broke down over the issue of security cameras and to reopen the trade route to China. The MNDAA attacked TNLA-controlled Kutkai using drones and took the city on 16 March.

descriptions: Both are members of the Northern Alliance, a coalition of insurgent groups.
edges.evidence_quote: the Northern Alliance —which consists of four insurgent groups , the Arakan Army (AA), the Kachin Independence Army (KIA), the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA) and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA)

descriptions: MNDAA and TNLA conduct joint attacks against the Tatmadaw.
edges.evidence_quote: The MNDAA and Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA) have carried out several joint attacks against the Tatmadaw in Shan State.

descriptions: After conflict, MNDAA and TNLA pledged friendship and cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: On the 17th anniversary of the founding of the Arakan Army, the MNDAA and TNLA pledged to continue their friendship, as well as cooperate with the AA and to work together until the 'revolutionary vision' is achieved.

descriptions: Northern Alliance includes MNDAA as a member.
edges.evidence_quote: The Northern Alliance is a coalition of four rebel groups – the MNDAA, AA, TNLA, and the KIA – that was founded in 2016

descriptions: The Three Brotherhood Alliance is composed of the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army as a member.
edges.evidence_quote: The Three Brotherhood Alliance ... is an alliance between the Arakan Army, Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army, and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army formed in June 2019.

descriptions: UWSA maintains close links with MNDAA.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA maintains close links with FPNCC partners like the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA)
